body.title: Inference router: migrate consumers to resolve(); retire (tier->model->provider) triples + ModelSpec.source_name
body.intention: I intend that every inference consumer dispatches through the dimensional resolver and no hardcoded (task_class, model_id, source_name) triple or 1:1 ModelSpec.source_name binding remains.
body.description:
The CUTOVER (do LAST, after resolver-compose is green) of D-inference-router-stack-decomposition-2026-07-08. Additive-then-cutover: only now do we switch consumers and delete the monolith.

**Affected files:** unseen_university/devices/inference/device.py (proxy entry -> resolve()); unseen_university/devices/igor/cognition/{consolidation,distillation}.py + tools/after_action_reviewer.py (any direct route() callers -> dimensional API); unseen_university/devices/scraps/jobs/routing_compiler.py (emit dimensional POLICY, not triples) + its add_compiled_rule counterpart in rules_engine.py; rules_engine.py (remove _DEFAULT_RULES triples + the triple form of add_compiled_rule); models_registry.py (remove the source_name binding); tests updated across tests/inference/.
**Design rules:** inference-proxy-only; inference-tier-not-model; docs-live-in-code; test-plan-or-why-not.
**Scope boundary:** repoint consumers + compiler to the dimensional API, THEN delete triples + source_name. HIGH-INERTIA (device.py, load-bearing proxy Igor consumes) — pause for Akien inline pre-approval before editing device.py; stamp approval into this ticket.
**Test plan:** device.py dispatch resolves via resolve() end-to-end; a live dispatch returns text (proxy up throughout); `git grep` finds NO (task_class, model_id, source_name) triple in the rules stack and no ModelSpec.source_name; full tests/inference/ + tests/granny/ green. Red (triples still present) -> green (removed, resolver serving).
**Rollback:** revert the cutover commit — the additive resolver + stacks remain; route()+triples were only deleted in this ticket, so revert restores the working monolith. No dispatch-down window.
**Logging:** log each consumer cutover crossing at INFO; log triple-removal as a state change.
**Depends on:** T-inference-resolver-compose.
**Observability:** a live dispatch returning text + the no-triple grep are the runtime signals the cutover shipped.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
body.result:
RE-CLOSED PROVEN after a self-caught bad close. First close (@441076bf) rested on a DEFECTIVE proof and is superseded by proof cc.0.the-inference-cutover-s-anti-confabulati @6704dc1d (red=AssertionError -> green=passed, parent-ref a25de650 = the bad close commit, so the red->green proves the FIX).

WHAT THE STRUCTURAL CUTOVER DID (stands): every consumer dispatches through the dimensional resolver; _DEFAULT_RULES triples + RoutingRule + route() + add_compiled_rule + _is_night_mode + by_source + seed_from_models + ModelSpec.source_name all DELETED. connections.py holds the authoritative 24-edge table (sole home of reachability) = 22 source_name bindings + the 2 triple-only owned-local Hex edges; phantom qwen/qwen3-30b-a3b-instruct@openrouter dropped. select() bridges task_class->ticket_tier -> resolve(); resolve() gained session affinity.

THE DEFECT I SHIPPED AND THEN CAUGHT: the triples hand-curated devstral-small-2:24b as the coding floor. Dimensional selection reads the STRUCTURED features flag, and devstral's was never populated even though its own catalog row already recorded 'agentic'/'tool-call' tags. With coding-needs-tools off (policies=[]) coding resolved to a tool-less REASONING model (deepseek-r1:14b) which cannot emit a tool call: it returned a JSON blob claiming "wrote smoke file" and wrote NOTHING. Confabulated success. Found only by RUNNING the live coding path and checking the filesystem -- the first proof never did, because its assertion died before the file check.

MY REASONING ERROR: I argued policies=[] "preserves the escalation ladder" because route()'s design rung returned deepseek-r1:32b. But deepseek-r1:32b also has features=[] and also cannot build -- I was matching an already-broken rung and calling it parity. Escalating a coding request past local capability now honestly returns None.

FIX: mirror the recorded capability into features=["tools"] on devstral (evidence-based; only devstral carries the tool-call tag, qwen3-coder-next does not and was NOT tagged), and wire device.py policies=None so coding-needs-tools is enforced. Coding resolves to devstral@ollama.

PROOF DEFECT ALSO FIXED: the first proof node built a live InferenceDevice() whose HealthMonitor probes real providers, so it passed on environment luck (Hex happened to be up) and asserted a WRONG invariant ("the design rung must serve"). The rewritten node is hermetic (no device) and asserts the invariant that was actually broken: a coding request must resolve to a tool-capable model, so a coding failure HALTS instead of silently claiming success. Plus: tag/feature mirror consistency, monolith-gone, Hex edges survive, every model reachable.

Suites: inference 436 passed, granny 101 passed. 3 pre-existing failures, all ticketed (T-preexisting-domain-prompt-sha, T-preexisting-repo-map-second-symbol, T-preexisting-dicksimnel-done-prefix-close). Two orthogonal bugs filed: T-inference-capability-ceiling-misclassified-as-availability (CP3 -- the walk calls a capability ceiling an availability outage, retries, and names the wrong cause; also triple-alarms), T-live-smoke-red-devstral-loop-not-routing (smoke red even with devstral correctly selected; the cutover never touched the loop/tools/prompts).
